# First-Agent

> Locally orchestrated, mixed-tier LLM coding agent for a single
> power-user. Filesystem-canonical knowledge (Markdown + YAML
> frontmatter); read-side scales lazily from grep to BM25 to
> vectors only when the corpus justifies it. Implementation language
> is Python; target codebases the agent reads can be anything.
>
> This file is an index for LLM agents that want to ingest the
> project documentation in one fetch. Paths below are GitHub raw URLs against
> the `main` branch of the **canonical repo**
> `GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent`. Forks
> `GrasshopperBoy/First-Agent-fork` and
> `Bupitsa-ai/First-Agent-debloat` are parallel work-trees — different
> agents run in different forks; cross-fork PRs land back upstream
> via the lead. Any of these is a valid working repo from an agent's
> perspective; `main` is kept in sync across all of them. If a URL
> below 404s mid-cycle, replace the canonical owner/repo segment with
> one of the fork pairs and retry.
>
> **Project stage.** Currently in **Stage 1** (documentation + agent
> development через Devin) of the three-stage evolution defined in
> [`knowledge/project-overview.md` §1.3](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/project-overview.md).

> **Tiered layout.** §MUST READ FIRST is the smallest path that
> bootstraps a session (six files, in order). §TASK ROUTING points
> task-types at the right primary entrypoint. §BY-DEMAND INDEX
> below is the full per-folder file list — open only the entries
> relevant to the task at hand.

## MUST READ FIRST

Six files cover ~90% of session-start context. Read in order:

1. [README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/README.md) — project overview (Russian).
2. [AGENTS.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md) — rules, PR checklist, query routing, **§Pre-flight checklist** (run BEFORE any non-trivial task).
3. [knowledge/project-overview.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/project-overview.md) — §1.1 four-pillar goal; §1.2 minimalism-first principle.
4. [HANDOFF.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/HANDOFF.md) — current state, next steps, recently-landed research notes.
5. [knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md) — one-paragraph cheat-sheet for all ADR + amendments. Authoritative source remains the per-ADR files.
6. [docs/glossary.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/docs/glossary.md) — canonical definitions for project-specific terms (axis, lens, pillar, NLAH, ACI, harness, hook, UC1..UC5, MCP, subtraction-first, minimalism-first, R-S-M).

## TASK ROUTING

Task type → primary entrypoint, then secondary:

- **Inner-loop / tool-registry contract** → [knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md) (canonical). Background: [research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md) (§0 Decision Briefing + §10 ADR-7 contract sketch — input note). Secondary: [research/cutting-edge-agent-research-radar-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/cutting-edge-agent-research-radar-2026-05.md), [research/semi-autonomous-agents-cross-reference-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/semi-autonomous-agents-cross-reference-2026-05.md).
- **New ADR or amendment** → [knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md) + [AGENTS.md PR Checklist rule #9](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md#pr-checklist) (must append a block to `exploration_log.md` and update `DIGEST.md` row in the same PR).
- **New module / Phase M PR** → [research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md) §9 roadmap + [ADR DIGEST](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md). Cite minimalism-first per [AGENTS.md PR Checklist rule #10](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md#pr-checklist).
- **Research-briefing note** → [knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md), then [knowledge/research/_template.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/_template.md) (§0 Decision Briefing pre-filled). Output rule: [AGENTS.md PR Checklist rule #8](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md#pr-checklist).
- **Edit `knowledge/trace/exploration_log.md`** → schema in [knowledge/README.md §`trace/`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/README.md); origin in [research/ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md) §9 R-1.
- **Module-touching PR — what CLASS / INVARIANT line to put in the PR description?** → [AGENTS.md §Change Classification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md#change-classification) (REPAIR / RELAX / WORKAROUND taxonomy + invariant declaration). Worked example of the WORKAROUND trap: [knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-001-spec-bypassing-workaround.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-001-spec-bypassing-workaround.md).
- **About to relocate / rename / restructure a module's filesystem artifact?** → grep [`knowledge/anti-patterns/`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/anti-patterns/README.md) for the symptom first; AP-001 is the canonical «path bypass to silence `git status`» trap.
- **Specific number / date / quote** → primary source from `source:` frontmatter, NOT a summary. Per [AGENTS.md §Query Routing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md#query-routing).

## BY-DEMAND INDEX

### Repo entrypoints

- [README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/README.md) (S, ~210 lines): Project overview, current status, roadmap (Russian).
- [AGENTS.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md) (M, ~530 lines): Instructions for AI agents working in this repo (English).
- [HANDOFF.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/HANDOFF.md) (M, ~740 lines): Cross-LLM session handoff — current state, next steps, bootstrap pointer (mirrors the Devin Knowledge note).

### Inner-loop runtime (`src/fa/inner_loop/`)

The materialised ADR-7 + ADR-8 runtime. Closed BACKLOG M-1 on 2026-05-20 in PR #24. Wave-2 stack #1 (R-2 + R-3 + R-6) lands in PR #25 (LoopGuard + FailureClassifier + attempt_history). Wave-2 stack #2 (R-4 + R-5 + R-34) lands in PR #26 (pre-tool BlockerMiddleware family + DSV YAML contracts + QA constants). Wave-3 stack #1 (R-45 + R-19) lands in PR-4 (cost guardian + eval-role family-disjointness role layer). Smoke entry-point: `fa inner-loop-smoke --workspace . --input README.md` (the `python -m fa.cli` form is a no-op because `fa/cli.py` has no `__main__` guard — always use the console-script entry point). Test suite under `tests/test_inner_loop_*.py` + `tests/test_hook_registry.py` + `tests/test_verifier.py` + `tests/test_cost_guardian.py` + `tests/test_roles.py`.

- `src/fa/inner_loop/registry.py` — `ToolRegistry` + `ToolSpec` / `ToolResult` / `ToolCall` dataclasses; per-call JSON-Schema Draft 2020-12 validation against `ToolSpec.input_schema` (ADR-7 §5). `dispatch()` returns `ToolResult.fail("invalid_params", ..., retryable=True)` instead of raising, so the loop can record it as a normal tool failure. Unexpected handler exceptions are wrapped as `ToolResult.fail("internal_error", ..., retryable=False)` so a crashing handler still produces a paired `tool_result` audit row.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/loop.py` — deterministic `run_session(calls, registry, hooks, state, limits)` driver. Reads `max_iterations` from `RuntimeLimits` (default 6, ADR-7 §Amendment 2026-05-20 rule 1). Wires every guard/observer step into `state.log` as a `kind="hook_decision"` row via the HookRegistry event-sink. `BETWEEN_ROUNDS` fires at the start of every iteration **including iteration 1** (ADR-8 Amendment 2026-05-20b) so session-level guards (PauseGuard, LoopGuard) can block the very first tool call.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/runtime_limits.py` — `RuntimeLimits` config loader; parses the `runtime_limits:` block in `~/.fa/config.yaml`, warns on unknown keys / non-positive values, falls back to anchored defaults if the file is absent. Fields: `max_iterations`, `bash_timeout_seconds`, `loop_guard_repeat_warn` / `_circuit_breaker` / `_window` (R-2), `attempt_history_max_entries` / `_max_age_seconds` (R-6), `qa_max_iterations` / `qa_max_consecutive_errors` / `qa_recurring_issue_threshold` (R-34, documented defaults for a future QA orchestrator), `rate_limit_suppression_seconds` / `lockfile_suppression_seconds` / `auth_expired_suppression_seconds` (R-4 BlockerMiddleware suppression windows), `cost_budget_usd` (R-45 cost-guardian; tri-mode `None` unbounded / `0.0` observe-only / `> 0` hard cap; parsed as `float` per the `_FLOAT_KEYS` set, every other knob is int).
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/base.py` — `HookRegistry`, `LifecyclePoint`, `GuardMiddleware` / `ObserverMiddleware`, `Decision` (allow / deny / modify / observed), `HookPayload`, `DispatchRecord`, `HookDecisionSink`. First-deny short-circuit, double-mutation hard error, family-disjoint enforcement at `register()`, and `revalidates_after_modify` replay for sandbox-shape guards.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/builtin.py` — `PauseGuard` (pause sentinel), `CapabilityGuard` (capability flags), `SandboxHook` (gates `fs.run_bash` + `fs.read_file` + `fs.write_file` via workspace-root containment), `ApprovalHook`, `AuditHook` (persists to `EventLog` when wired), `VerifierObserver`, `LearningObserver` (R-8 filesystem-canon writer, wired into `fa inner-loop-smoke`; smoke and the T-2 real runtime share the **single canon root** `<workspace>/knowledge/trace/{codebase_map.json,gotchas.md}`; live-repo cleanliness across repeated smoke runs comes from fixed-clock injection on `LearningObserver.now="2026-05-21T00:00:00Z"` plus byte-suffix dedup in `record_gotcha` plus a seed `knowledge/trace/codebase_map.json` baseline checked into the repo and asserted by `tests/test_cli.py::test_inner_loop_smoke_canon_snapshot_matches_seed_baseline`; T-2 omits `now` for live wall-clock provenance; discovery key is path-keyed `"{tool/slug}/{path}"` or `"{tool/slug}/{call_id}"` fallback via `_learning_observer_key`; write failures surface as `hook_decision` / `observer_error_swallowed` rows), `default_tool_result_for_denial`.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/loop_guard.py` — **R-2** `LoopGuard` `GuardMiddleware` at `BEFORE_TOOL_EXEC` + `BETWEEN_ROUNDS`. Two non-progress detectors over a sliding window: (1) identical-call repeat (`params_hash` collision) at `loop_guard_repeat_warn` → warn / `_circuit_breaker` → deny; (2) same-path thrash (distinct `params_hash`es targeting the same `path` hint) at the same thresholds. Deny propagates through `run_session`'s `BETWEEN_ROUNDS` `try/except PermissionError` and writes a `run_stopped` audit row.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/blockers.py` — **R-4** `BlockerMiddleware` family (`RateLimitBlocker`, `LockfileBlocker`, `AuthExpiredBlocker`). Each is a `GuardMiddleware` attached to both `BEFORE_TOOL_EXEC` (gate) and `AFTER_TOOL_EXEC` (observe). Pattern-detection lives in subclass `_detect(ToolResult) -> bool`; gating is keyed by `tool_name` + `suppression_seconds` from `RuntimeLimits`. `AuthExpiredBlocker.suppression_seconds == 0` by default = observe-only (synthetic re-auth lands with T-2 LLM driver). Dormant on baseline tools — `hook_deny` / `read_failed` / `write_failed` / `command_failed` / `command_timeout` / `invalid_params` / `internal_error` don't match the rate-limit / lockfile / auth-expired signatures.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/hooks/recovery_observers.py` — **R-3** observers wired at `AFTER_TOOL_EXEC`. `RecoveryActionObserver` calls `classify_result(ToolResult) → RecoveryAction` and emits a `kind="recovery_action"` audit row (one per failure). `AttemptHistoryObserver` appends the same failure to `attempt_history.json` for the coder-recovery reader prompt.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/recovery/` — pure data layer for R-3 + R-6 (no I/O coupling to hooks). `classify.py` is a deterministic `ToolError → RecoveryAction` table (code first, keyword fallback); `attempt_history.py` is the per-run JSON writer (sliding window + age cap from `RuntimeLimits`).
- `src/fa/inner_loop/state.py` — `SessionState` + `EventLog`. ADR-7 §7 schema: `event_id` / `ts` / `run_id` / `actor` / `kind` / `content` / `tool_name` / `tool_call_id` / `parent_event_id` / `harness_id`. JSONL writer is single-file-per-run; R-8 `LearningObserver` is explicitly outside this event-kind list because it writes `knowledge/trace/` filesystem artifacts instead.
- `src/fa/inner_loop/tools/` — `build_baseline_registry(workspace_root, bash_timeout_seconds=...)` returns the three M-1 tools (`fs.read_file`, `fs.write_file`, `fs.run_bash`). Bash timeout flows from `RuntimeLimits.bash_timeout_seconds`.
- `src/fa/verifier/` — **R-5** DSV (Deterministic State Verification) module. `VerifierContract` / `TraceEvent` / `VerificationResult` dataclasses; `verify_action(contract, events)` checks `required_trace_events` + `failure_conditions`; `load_contract(yaml_text)` parses a single contract; `load_contracts_from_dir(directory)` batch-loads `verifiers/*.yaml` files keyed by in-file `target_action`. Missing directory returns empty mapping (smoke CLI runs before contracts are vendored).
- `verifiers/` — vendored DSV contracts for the three M-1 tools (`fs.read_file.yaml`, `fs.write_file.yaml`, `fs.run_bash.yaml`) plus the documentation-anchor `edit_file.yaml`. `required_trace_events` is empty in M-1 (tool bodies don't yet emit per-step trace events — T-2 lands observation-event projection); `failure_conditions` enumerate the canonical `ToolResult.error.code` values per tool. `override_action: force_failure` is the contract's directive to the future T-2 consumer when a check trips.

### Observability (`src/fa/observability/`)

**R-45 cost guardian** — dormant on M-1 baseline tools, wakes when the T-2 LLM driver emits `cost=tokens_in=<int>,tokens_out=<int>,usd=<float>` artifacts in `ToolResult.artifacts`. Documented in ADR-7 §Sub-amendment 2026-05-21 (event-kind schema) and Q-7 sub-amendment in `knowledge/trace/exploration_log.md`.

- `src/fa/observability/cost_guardian.py` — single `CostGuardian(GuardMiddleware)` class attaching to both `BEFORE_TOOL_EXEC` (gates when `RuntimeLimits.cost_budget_usd` exceeded) and `AFTER_TOOL_EXEC` (parses `cost=…` artifacts via `default_cost_extractor`, accumulates per-session `CostRollup`, writes `kind="cost_observation"` audit rows when an `EventLog` is wired). Ships `CostObservation` + `CostRollup` value dataclasses and the `CostExtractor` protocol (custom extractors may be injected for non-default artifact shapes).
- `src/fa/observability/__init__.py` — public exports: `COST_ARTIFACT_PREFIX`, `CostExtractor`, `CostGuardian`, `CostObservation`, `CostRollup`, `default_cost_extractor`.

### Role-config helpers (`src/fa/roles.py`)

**R-19 eval-role family-disjointness (role layer)** — pure-function helpers for the `~/.fa/models.yaml` loader (loader lands with the T-2 LLM driver). Complement to the hook-layer call site in ADR-7 §Amendment 2026-05-20 rule 4. Documented in ADR-2 §Sub-amendment 2026-05-21 and Q-2 sub-amendment in `knowledge/trace/exploration_log.md`.

- `src/fa/roles.py` — `KNOWN_FAMILIES` frozenset (10 entries: anthropic, openai, google, qwen, deepseek, glm, kimi, mimo, nemotron, llama). `extract_family(slug, *, override=None) -> str` runs a regex slug-to-family table; raises `FamilyExtractionError` on ambiguous slugs (caller falls back to explicit `family:` override in `~/.fa/models.yaml`). `check_eval_disjoint(*, planner_family, coder_family, eval_family) -> None` raises `EvalFamilyConflictError` (`ValueError` subclass) when eval shares family with planner or coder; planner and coder are permitted to share a family per ADR-2 §Decision routing table.

### Docs (`docs/`)

- [docs/README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/docs/README.md) (S, ~30 lines): Wiki index.
- [docs/architecture.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/docs/architecture.md) (S, ~240 lines): Three-layer agent architecture (Interface / Cognitive / Execution).
- [docs/workflow.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/docs/workflow.md) (S, ~170 lines): R-S-M lifecycle, Devin session conventions, and parallel-agent fork coordination.
- [docs/prompting.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/docs/prompting.md) (S, ~160 lines): T1-T5 prompt templates and anti-patterns.
- [docs/glossary.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/docs/glossary.md) (S, ~70 lines): Project glossary.

*Archived 2026-05-08 (in-place stubs, excluded from this routing surface): `docs/devin-reference.md` (Devin-specific — restored once `llms.txt` learns to gate by agent host), `docs/agent-creation-github.md` (external-tutorial transcript; live ADR-7 inputs are `research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md` and `research/how-to-build-an-agent-ampcode-2026-04.md`).*

### Project knowledge (`knowledge/`)

- [knowledge/README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/README.md) (S, ~220 lines): Knowledge folder conventions, frontmatter schema, query routing.
- [knowledge/project-overview.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/project-overview.md) (M, ~450 lines): Product/scope snapshot for v0.1; §1.1 four-pillar goal (research-backed implementation + pragmatic single-user + most efficient open-source harness + iteration-via-measurement); §1.2 minimalism-first principle; §1.3 three-stage project evolution.
- [knowledge/MAINTENANCE.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/MAINTENANCE.md) (S, ~190 lines): Maintenance / cross-reference / sweep-cadence rules — single canonical list of «touch these files when archiving / superseding / amending». Companion to `BACKLOG.md`; referenced from AGENTS.md PR Checklist rule #5.
- [knowledge/BACKLOG.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/BACKLOG.md) (M, ~590 lines): Deferred-ideas tracking with unblock-triggers — nine entries I-1..I-9: harness-component deferrals (I-1 planner-picks-skills, I-2 sub-agents, I-3 dispatcher LLM); process items (I-4 EXEMPT scope, I-5 RESOLVER T2-T5 templates); tooling and measurement deferrals (I-6 llms.txt pre-commit regenerator, I-7 bootstrap-cost auto-collected KPI, I-8 mid-tier × FA-own-harness re-test); playbook conversion (I-9 repo-audit playbook → SKILL.md). Each entry names the concrete artefact whose existence moves the idea from «deferred» to «actionable».

### ADRs (`knowledge/adr/`)

- [knowledge/adr/README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/README.md) (S, ~30 lines): ADR process; routes to DIGEST.md cheat-sheet.
- [knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/DIGEST.md) (M, ~370 lines): One-paragraph cheat-sheet per accepted ADR + amendments. Cheap-read overlay; authoritative source remains the per-ADR file. Updated in the same PR whenever an amendment lands (AGENTS.md PR Checklist rule #9).
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-template.md) (S, ~40 lines): ADR template (copy when adding a new decision).
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-1-v01-use-case-scope.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-1-v01-use-case-scope.md) (S, ~230 lines): v0.1 use-case scope (UC1 + UC3 in; UC4 deferred; UC2 best-effort); Amendment 2026-05-01 deferred UC5; Amendment 2026-05-06 expanded UC5 to eval-driven harness iteration (5a-5e).
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-2-llm-tiering.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-2-llm-tiering.md) (M, ~720 lines): LLM tiering and access (static role routing; Planner / Coder / Debug / Eval).
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-3-memory-architecture-variant.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-3-memory-architecture-variant.md) (S, ~150 lines): Memory architecture variant for v0.1 (Variant A "Mechanical Wiki").
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-4-storage-backend.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-4-storage-backend.md) (S, ~220 lines): Storage backend for v0.1 (SQLite FTS5).
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-5-chunker-tool.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-5-chunker-tool.md) (S, ~280 lines): Chunker tool selection for v0.1 (universal-ctags + markdown-it-py).
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-6-tool-sandbox-allow-list.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-6-tool-sandbox-allow-list.md) (M, ~670 lines): Tool sandbox & path allow-list policy for v0.1 (deny-by-default read+write allow-list at `~/.fa/sandbox.toml`). **§Amendment 2026-05-20 (Wave-1)** adds the three-layer bash sandbox gate (`src/fa/sandbox/{classifier,validators,path_containment,bash_gate}.py`) — pattern classifier (5 categories) + per-command validators (`rm`/`chmod`/`git`) + symlink-resolved path containment + `evaluate_bash` composer. Ported from Aperant `bash-validator.ts`+`path-containment.ts` and Gortex `bash_classify.go`.
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md) (L, ~1490 lines): Inner-loop & tool-registry contract for v0.1 (MCP-shaped `ToolSpec`/`ToolResult`; five-tool `fs.*` catalog; two edit-shapes; JSON-Schema validation; three-tier tool disclosure; trace separation `events.jsonl` ≠ `hot.md`; mini hook pipeline `pre_tool` + `post_tool` only; static layered prompt; 4-question subtraction-first acceptance block; §Amendment 2026-05-12 cross-references `bootstrap-cost-baseline-2026-05.md` empirical evidence).
- [knowledge/adr/ADR-8-hook-registry.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-8-hook-registry.md) (M, ~450 lines): HookRegistry middleware-chain contract (doc-first; runtime BACKLOG M-1). Five lifecycle points (`BETWEEN_ROUNDS`, `BEFORE_LLM_CALL`, `AFTER_LLM_CALL`, `BEFORE_TOOL_EXEC`, `AFTER_TOOL_EXEC`); two middleware kinds (`GuardMiddleware` may deny/modify, `ObserverMiddleware` read-only); dispatcher first-deny short-circuit; family-disjoint rule enforced at `register()`; migration plan for v0.1 hooks. 8-project convergence cited (DPC + Gortex + 6 others). Source: `research/borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md` §R-1.

### Anti-pattern catalog (`knowledge/anti-patterns/`)

The durable catalog of named anti-patterns the project has either rejected on principle or actually committed and reverted. Each `AP-NNN-<slug>.md` is single-purpose: surface symptom, wrong shape, right shape, why the wrong shape dominates, detection layers, linked-ADR, and commit-level evidence. Complementary to [`AGENTS.md` §Cross-project anti-patterns](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md#cross-project-anti-patterns) (terse, external evidence, read every session) and to [`AGENTS.md` §Change Classification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/AGENTS.md#change-classification) (the Layer-1 forcing function the catalog backs up). Detector personas (R-32 §What) are deferred until ≥3 entries exist; the catalog is currently the passive-read surface.

- [knowledge/anti-patterns/README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/anti-patterns/README.md) (S, ~120 lines): catalog scope + entry schema (Symptom / Wrong shape / Right shape / Why-it-dominates / Detection / Linked-ADR / Evidence) + how a new entry lands + index table.
- [knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-001-spec-bypassing-workaround.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-001-spec-bypassing-workaround.md) (S, ~200 lines): «Spec-bypassing workaround masquerading as fix» — the R-8 wave-3 incident verbatim. Linked from [ADR-7 §Sub-amendment 2026-05-21b](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/adr/ADR-7-inner-loop-tool-registry.md#sub-amendment-2026-05-21b--r-8-learningobserver-filesystem-canon-artifacts) worked-history note. Test surface: `tests/test_cli.py::test_invariant_adr7_r8_canon_root_is_knowledge_trace` (Layer-2 worked example).
- [knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-002-stale-routing-index-counts.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/anti-patterns/AP-002-stale-routing-index-counts.md) (S, ~190 lines): «Stale row counts in routing index drift silently» — the M2 sweep evidence that 16/58 llms.txt rows had >10-LOC drift and 3 rows shifted bucket. Linked from [`knowledge/MAINTENANCE.md` §When adding a new file](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/MAINTENANCE.md#when-adding-a-new-file-under-docs-or-knowledge); right-shape is the bucket overlay (S/M/L/XL @ 300/800/1500) introduced in this PR.

### Exploration log (`knowledge/trace/`)

- [knowledge/trace/exploration_log.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/trace/exploration_log.md) (L, ~1030 lines): Telegraphic markdown overlay — one `## Q-N` block per accepted ADR with `Chosen:` and each `Rejected:` option carrying `Reason:` + `Lesson:`. Schema in [`knowledge/README.md` §`trace/`](./README.md#trace--exploration-log); origin in research note [`ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md`](./research/ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md) §9 R-1. Converted from YAML DAG 2026-05-10 per Tsinghua NLAH finding.

### Prompts (`knowledge/prompts/`)

- [knowledge/prompts/README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/README.md) (S, ~60 lines): Prompts library conventions and index.
- [knowledge/prompts/RESOLVER.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/RESOLVER.md) (S, ~50 lines): Intent-to-template dispatcher (T1-T5 plus role prompts).
- [knowledge/prompts/research-topic.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/research-topic.md) (S, ~40 lines): T1 research-topic task prompt.
- [knowledge/prompts/architect-fa.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/architect-fa.md) (M, ~500 lines): Architect / Planner system prompt (full).
- [knowledge/prompts/architect-fa-compact.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/architect-fa-compact.md) (S, ~210 lines): Architect / Planner system prompt (compact).
- [knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/research-briefing.md) (S, ~230 lines): Research-briefing workflow — goal-lens elicitation, deep-dive note with §0 Decision Briefing, chat handover. Use for cross-reference / paper-vs-architecture reviews.
- [knowledge/prompts/repo-audit-playbook.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/repo-audit-playbook.md) (L, ~970 lines): 7-phase playbook for agent-oriented repo audits — P1 inventory → P2 finding extraction → P3 routing-surface check → P4 self-review → P5 effect assessment → P6 critical re-pass (four-part filter) → P7 capture-as-workflow. Load only when the user requests a repo-audit-style refactor; do not load on every session. Future skill conversion tracked as BACKLOG I-9.
- [knowledge/prompts/handoff-summarizer.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/handoff-summarizer.md) (S, ~100 lines): System-prompt fragment for session-handoff / compaction tasks. Forces identifier-verbatim preservation (UUIDs, file paths, PR numbers, decisions) and a 5-block structure (Context / Decisions / Progress / Pending / Data). Use when writing `HANDOFF.md`, an inner-loop compaction (~100 k-token ceiling per AGENTS.md rule #11), or any LLM-to-LLM session-end summary. FA equivalent of Kronos's identity-preserving compaction prompt — see [`research/kronos-agent-os-inspiration-2026-05.md`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/kronos-agent-os-inspiration-2026-05.md) §0 R-5.
- [knowledge/prompts/coder-recovery.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/coder-recovery.md) (S, ~100 lines): Coder-role retry prefix prompt (Wave-2 R-6 reader half). Forces the model to count prior `(tool, params_hash)` matches in `attempt_history.json` and articulate a concrete differentiator on N≥1, or escalate on N≥2. Paired with `fa.inner_loop.hooks.AttemptHistoryObserver` (writer) and `fa.inner_loop.hooks.FailureClassifierObserver` (category source). See [`research/borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md) §R-6 and Aperant `coder_recovery.md` (Part 2 item 13).
- [knowledge/prompts/tool-shapes.yaml](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/prompts/tool-shapes.yaml) (S, ~90 lines): Per-tier tool-shape registry (R-18 / ADR-2 §Amendment 2026-05-20 Wave-1). One entry per LLM family currently routed in ADR-2 tier table (anthropic / openai / qwen / deepseek / glm / kimi) with `family:` / `shape.edit:` / `shape.tool_call_format:` / `handoff_one_liner:`. Read-only metadata; harness injects the *previous* role's one-liner into the *next* role's prompt on role-switch.

### Research (`knowledge/research/`)

> Convention: rows for notes produced via the research-briefing
> workflow lead with a `[goal: <one-sentence>]` prefix so an agent
> reading this index can filter by current task without loading the
> note. Older rows can be migrated lazily (rule #8 in AGENTS.md is
> forward-only).

- [knowledge/research/_template.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/_template.md) (S, ~110 lines): Skeleton for new research notes — frontmatter v1+v2 + `goal_lens:` + §0 Decision Briefing pre-filled. Pair with `prompts/research-briefing.md`.
- [knowledge/research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/memory-architecture-design-2026-04-26.md) (L, ~950 lines): Memory architecture roadmap, three variants, open questions. Source for ADR-3.
- [knowledge/research/llm-wiki-critique.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/llm-wiki-critique.md) (M, ~350 lines): Cross-cutting critique of LLM-Wiki / GraphRAG approaches.
- [knowledge/research/socraticode-code-intelligence-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/socraticode-code-intelligence-2026-05.md) (M, ~630 lines): [goal: Identify what First-Agent can use, benefit from, or learn from SocratiCode without violating v0.1 scope or license constraints.] Cross-reference of SocratiCode's MCP code-intelligence engine against First-Agent ADR-1..6; recommends context-artifact manifests, local RRF fusion, resumable indexing contracts, graph-compatible IDs, prompt-level search discipline, and an AGPL license firewall.

*Archived 2026-05-08 (in-place stubs, excluded from this routing surface; open the file directly for git-history / audit — banner at top points to the live superseder): `agent-roles.md` (→ ADR-2 + DIGEST.md), `agent-video-research.md` (→ `research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md`), `architect-fa-refactor-ru.md` (→ `knowledge/prompts/architect-fa{,-compact}.md`), `llm-wiki-community-batch-{1,2}.md` (→ ADR-3 + DIGEST.md), `llm-wiki-critique-sources.md` and `llm-wiki-critique-first-agent.md` (→ `research/llm-wiki-critique.md`), `ai-context-os-memm-deep-dive.md` and `agentic-memory-supplement.md` (→ ADR-3 + DIGEST.md), `cross-reference-ampcode-sliders-to-adr-2026-04.md` (→ ADR-2/4/5/6 amendments + `research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md` §10).*

- [knowledge/research/chunker-design.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/chunker-design.md) (M, ~770 lines): Chunker design — universal-ctags vs tree-sitter vs per-language regex; recommendation for ADR-5.
- [knowledge/research/how-to-build-an-agent-ampcode-2026-04.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/how-to-build-an-agent-ampcode-2026-04.md) (M, ~520 lines): Разбор статьи Thorsten Ball / Amp «How to Build an Agent» — inner-loop, tool registry, 3 базовых tool'а (read_file / list_files / edit_file); mapping на ADR-1/ADR-2 и UC1.
- [knowledge/research/sliders-structured-reasoning-2026-04.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/sliders-structured-reasoning-2026-04.md) (M, ~730 lines): Разбор препринта SLIDERS (Stanford OV AL, arXiv:2604.22294) — aggregation-bottleneck, contextualized chunking, schema induction, relevance gate, primary-key reconciliation, SQL-QA; mapping на ADR-3 / ADR-4 / ADR-5 и UC2 / UC3.
- [knowledge/research/ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/ara-protocol-cross-reference-2026-05.md) (L, ~1170 lines): Cross-reference review Ara protocol (arXiv:2604.24658v2, Orchestra Research) против ADR-1..6 + HANDOFF.md — mapping four layers / Live Research Manager / Compiler / Seal на текущие artefact'ы FA, риски наивного заимствования, 10 пронумерованных рекомендаций (R-1..R-10) и 11 открытых вопросов под решение лида.
- [knowledge/research/semi-autonomous-agents-cross-reference-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/semi-autonomous-agents-cross-reference-2026-05.md) (L, ~1370 lines): Критический разбор трёх источников по semi-autonomous LLM-агентам (deep-research-report, semi-autonomous-llm-agents research, репо `nextlevelbuilder/goclaw`) против ADR-1..6 — accept/defer/reject фильтрация; основа для ADR-2 §Amendment 2026-05-01 (MCP forward-compat) и ADR-1 §Amendment 2026-05-01 (UC5 deferred); input для будущего ADR-7 inner-loop (ACI principle, hooks primitive).
- [knowledge/research/cutting-edge-agent-research-radar-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/cutting-edge-agent-research-radar-2026-05.md) (M, ~640 lines): Радар cutting-edge исследований для First-Agent v0.1/v0.2 — MCP/tool registry, ACI, hooks, memory, eval traces, sandbox/audit и multi-agent coordination; backlog input для ADR-7 prep и будущих module PRs.
- [knowledge/research/agent-ui-research-radar-v0-2-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/agent-ui-research-radar-v0-2-2026-05.md) (M, ~660 lines): Agent UI radar for First-Agent v0.2 — Hermes Agent UI implementations, Pi surfaces/packages, OpenClaw gateway/UI forks, and human-agent UI research; backlog input for future UI/control-plane ADR prep.
- [knowledge/research/latent-verifier-evolve-research-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/latent-verifier-evolve-research-2026-05.md) (M, ~550 lines): [goal: Form a list of research artifacts that strengthen First-Agent and identify source ideas worth carrying forward.] Corrective research-briefing note on Latent Space Survey, CUA Verifiers, Squeeze Evolve, and CUAVerifierBench vs ADR-1..6 — §0 Decision Briefing with 6 recommendations: acceptance rubric fixtures, per-session tool registry, trace/eval schema, latent-space watch-list, gated dynamic routing, CUAVerifierBench as schema reference.
- [knowledge/research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/efficient-llm-agent-harness-2026-05.md) (L, ~1010 lines): [goal: Подготовить решения для ADR-7 inner-loop / tool-contract — эффективный First-Agent v0.1 harness без расширения v0.1 scope, с явным cross-reference на ADR-1..6 и primary-source-cited evidence base.] Single source-of-truth research-briefing note for ADR-7 prep объединяющая две ранние редакции (upstream PR #37 + PR #38, обе close-without-merge); §0 Decision Briefing с девятью recommendations (R-1..R-9, 8 TAKE + 1 DEFER, 0 surviving UNCERTAIN-ASK), §10 ADR-7 contract sketch (ToolSpec / ToolResult / Trace + R-8 static-layered-prompt invariant + R-7 subtraction-first acceptance-block).
- [knowledge/research/soviet-code-inspiration-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/soviet-code-inspiration-2026-05.md) (L, ~990 lines): [goal: Извлечь архитектурные паттерны из reference-репо для инспирации FA inner-loop + knowledge layer.] Deep-dive in `Disentinel/soviet-code` (two-layer Claude-Code wrapper, npm-published v1.964.0): 22 patterns across STALIN-pipeline + conductor daemon + IDEOLOGY/KADRY/CLAUDE.md docs, §11 addendum with 10 more patterns (production Telegram bridge, two-stream JSONL logging, Komissar Naikan reflection, NEP technical-debt pragma, anti-pattern catalog + on-demand detectors). §0 Decision Briefing with 4 R-N (B-NEW-1..4): declarative tool whitelist (TAKE), mandatory Inspection (TAKE), heartbeat tick + Komissar Naikan (DEFER), anti-pattern catalog (DEFER).
- [knowledge/research/chat-cross-reference-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/chat-cross-reference-2026-05.md) (M, ~330 lines): [goal: Извлечь из чат-логов имена/проекты/паттерны для cross-reference в будущих сессиях; собрать reading-list.] Cross-reference list compiled from two chat extracts (pre-ADR-6 + post-ADR-7): 10 participants, 27 patterns in 5 groups (Knowledge/Memory, Tools/Code-analysis, Agent-design, Identity/Trust, Meta), 6 reference repos with first-pass priority (`Disentinel/soviet-code`, `mikhashev/dpc-messenger`, `AndyMik90/Aperant`, `zzet/gortex`, `spyrae/kronos-agent-os`, `makroumi/ulmen`), §9 glossary expanding FA ADR/BACKLOG/rule#10 references. §0 Decision Briefing with 3 R-N: reading-list catalog (TAKE), 6-concept FA mapping (TAKE), tool-call hallucination watchdog as BACKLOG candidate (UNCERTAIN-ASK).
- [knowledge/research/dpc-messenger-inspiration-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/dpc-messenger-inspiration-2026-05.md) (L, ~1130 lines): [goal: Извлечь из DPC-Messenger ADR-стека + inner-loop кода паттерны для FA HookRegistry / KG / sleep consolidation.] Deep-dive in `mikhashev/dpc-messenger` (npm v0.14+, 26 ADRs over 14 months, ~72K LOC Python core + 29K UI): 24 patterns in 5 groups, §0 Decision Briefing with 6 R-N including 3 B-NEW candidates (B-NEW-5 HookRegistry+2-tier middleware = TAKE; B-NEW-6 bi-temporal KG = DEFER; B-NEW-7 sleep consolidation + _meta.json = DEFER) and 3 citation rules for AGENTS.md (ADR-015 evolution-removed, ADR-005 P18 scope-only-estimation, ADR-021 Lesson 4 write-only-subsystems). Russian footnotes added to all 24 patterns + 8 sections for readability.
- [knowledge/research/gortex-aperant-inspiration-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/gortex-aperant-inspiration-2026-05.md) (M, ~560 lines): [goal: Извлечь из gortex (code-intelligence + agent config audit) и Aperant (role-routing prompts + filesystem learning) паттерны для FA Mechanical Wiki + role-routing.] Research-only brief comparing gortex (Go-native code-intelligence daemon + MCP server, 15 AI agent integrations, 62 MCP tools, 256 languages) and Aperant (Electron planner/coder/qa pipeline + GitHub PR review automation). §0 Decision Briefing with 5 R-N: bloat/staleness audit lifted from gortex `internal/audit/` (TAKE), per-agent config emission adapter pattern (DEFER), role-routing prompts library from Aperant (TAKE selective), filesystem-canonical learning loop `record_discovery`/`record_gotcha` (TAKE), phase-protocol orchestration (DEFER until Phase M).
- [knowledge/research/correlated-llm-errors-and-ensembling-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/correlated-llm-errors-and-ensembling-2026-05.md) (L, ~1250 lines): [goal: Снизить session-start context noise для будущих агентов + найти один immediate-improvement, implementable в следующий PR (combined (a)+(b) per research-briefing.md Stage 1).] Cross-reference review of 4 arxiv papers (P-1 Cornell correlated LLM errors 2506.07962, P-2 Simula multi-model ensembling 2510.21513, P-3 Nitarach AIMO 3 "Model Capability Dominates" inference-time optimization 2603.27844, P-4 Stanford "Theory of Generalization in Deep Learning" NTK theory 2605.01172) against ADR-1/2/7 and existing FA research notes; full §0 Decision Briefing with multiple R-N covering tier-redundancy implications, gated-ensemble retry, judge-correlation watch-list.
- [knowledge/research/kronos-agent-os-inspiration-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/kronos-agent-os-inspiration-2026-05.md) (M, ~690 lines): [goal: Извлечь из spyrae/kronos-agent-os (Python agent OS, ~9.7K LOC, SQLite-canonical, capability-gate-first) паттерны для FA Mechanical Wiki / inner-loop / B-NEW-* candidates.] Deep-dive в `spyrae/kronos-agent-os` (KAOS): 4 оси (Memory: SQLite+FTS5+Qdrant+KG; Skills; Swarm; Capability gates) + adjacent patterns appendix. §0 Decision Briefing с 6 R-N: SafeDB lock-serialized SQLite substrate (SKIP — ADR-4 stdlib sqlite3 sufficient), memory v0.2 enrichment Ebbinghaus tiering + audit JSONL redaction (DEFER), loop-detector 3-detector × 3-level circuit-breaker (TAKE для Phase M), filesystem-canonical skill store + safe community import (TAKE — unblocks BACKLOG I-9, 3-project convergence с DPC B-NEW-7 + Aperant R-4), identity-preserving compaction prompt (TAKE — 5-line prompt fragment для HANDOFF.md), cron-scheduled self-improvement + conversational approval (SKIP per BACKLOG I-7/I-8 Prior-art DPC ADR-015).
- [knowledge/research/borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md) (XL, ~1840 lines): [goal: Извлечь из 7 research-нот concrete actionable list для FA Phase M implementation; bypass research → ADR → impl cycle через targeted borrowing.] Inspiration-consolidation index (compiled 2026-05-13). 30 R-N candidates organised in 5 implementation waves (0..4): Wave 0 (low-cost docs, 13 R-Ns), Wave 1 (small Python + 1 security-sensitive port, 5 R-Ns), Wave 2 (HookRegistry-gated, 4 R-Ns), Wave 3+4 (sub-agent + MCP + UC5). Source pointers per R-N to gortex-aperant / correlated-llm-errors / dpc-messenger / chat-cross-reference / kronos-agent-os / external user borrow-list. **Canonical primary source for all `borrow-roadmap-2026-05.md §R-N` citations in ADR amendments, glossary entries, Python module docstrings, HANDOFF.md, DIGEST.md, exploration_log.md added 2026-05-20** (Wave-0+Wave-1 PRs #18/#19/#20).
- [knowledge/research/bootstrap-cost-baseline-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/bootstrap-cost-baseline-2026-05.md) (M, ~310 lines): Measurement-evidence note (не research-briefing — §0 exempt per AGENTS.md rule #8 forward-only). Первая quantitative baseline для bootstrap-cost после 2026-05 readability refactor: семь сессий (3 Devin ADR-7-prep + 1 Devin meta + 3 Arena.ai Agent Mode), **6-файловый irreducible** routing-compliant core воспроизводится во всех шести ADR-7-prep сессиях независимо от model selection и agent harness; bootstrap-floor across harnesses 9 calls / 8 files / ~70 K (Arena), Devin-only floor 24 calls / 7 files / ~95 K (Opus); re-measurement triggers зафиксированы в §9, items 5-6 cross-link на BACKLOG I-7 / I-8. Первый persistent datapoint для project-overview.md §1.1 Pillar 4 (iteration via measurement).
- [knowledge/research/correlated-llm-errors-and-ensembling-2026-05.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GITcrassuskey-shop/First-Agent/main/knowledge/research/correlated-llm-errors-and-ensembling-2026-05.md) (L, ~1250 lines): [goal: Снизить session-start context noise для будущих агентов + найти один immediate-improvement, implementable в следующий PR (combined (a)+(b) per research-briefing.md Stage 1).] Cross-reference research-briefing note для четырёх arxiv papers (P-1 Cornell «Correlated Errors in LLMs» ICML 2025 / P-2 Simula «Wisdom and Delusion of LLM Ensembles for Code Generation and Repair» 2026 / P-3 Nitarach «Model Capability Dominates: AIMO 3 Inference Optimization» Apr 2026 / P-4 Stanford «A Theory of Generalization in Deep Learning» May 2026) против ADR-1/ADR-2/ADR-7 + AGENTS.md rule #10; §0 Decision Briefing с шестью основными recommendations (R-1 TAKE Eval-role provider/family disjointness ADR-2 amendment как immediate-PR, R-2 TAKE no-cross-tier-escalation rationale hardening, R-3 TAKE prompt-diversity-layer anti-pattern citation в DIGEST §See also, R-4 DEFER multi-model ensembling с diversity-selector → BACKLOG I-10 UC5d-blocked, R-5 DEFER cross-link с `latent-verifier-evolve` R-4, R-6 SKIP P-4 NTK generalization theory как out-of-scope для UC1+UC3 harness scope). **§10 Secondary lens (loop / sandbox / tool-calling, 2026-05-13 addendum)** — re-read тех же papers под non-primary goal_lens-2 даёт ещё три docs-only TAKE: R-7 ADR-7 retry-budget invariant из P-3 §4.3, R-8 intra-role retry temperature default T=1.0 из P-3 §4.1 (conditional, pending FA Coder workload replication per Q-2), R-9 LLM-using-hook constraint provider/family ≠ acting-role из P-1 §4 (symmetric к R-1); шесть CUT-findings задокументированы в §10.4 для transparency.
